title: Access control
description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.

title: Access granting process used
description: Implement a formal access granting process that ensures new access privileges are assigned based on the principle of least privilege, and require at least one employee to endorse the granting of new access.

title: Access management policy established
description: Systematic controls are established in the access management policy for managing user access rights, ensuring appropriate, authorized access to systems and data.

title: Access requests to sensitive data required
description: To access sensitive data, employees must submit access requests that are approved and monitored.

title: Access requests to sensitive infrastructure required
description: To access sensitive infrastructure components, employees must submit access requests that are approved and monitored. This control helps ensure that only authorized personnel can access critical infrastructure, minimizing potential risks.

title: Access revoking process enforced
description: Implement a regular access review process to promptly remove access privileges from employees who no longer require them. This ensures that former employees or users do not retain unauthorized access to organizational resources.

title: Access rights
description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified and removed in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.

title: Access to source code
description: Read and write access to source code, development tools and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.

title: Account inventory maintained
description: An inventory of user accounts on critical and high-risk vendors that have access to in-scope systems and services is maintained, including essential details such as account owners, access privileges, associated roles, and vendor relationships where applicable. The inventory is reviewed and updated at least annually.

title: Audit log access restricted
description: Ensure audit information and audit logging tools are safeguarded against unauthorized access, modification, and deletion. In order to maintain the integrity and reliability of audit information, access to audit logging functionality must be restricted to a limited subset of privileged users or roles that have an explicit business need.

title: Authentication information
description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on the appropriate handling of authentication information.

title: Authenticators initially and periodically tested
description: Initially and periodically test devices (tokens/cards) that bear or generate identification code/password information to ensure proper function and no unauthorized alteration.

title: Automated decision-making and profiling has oversight
description: This requirements establishes safeguards for automated decision-making processes, including profiling, to align with GDPR requirements and protect data subjects' rights.

title: Automatic user logoff enforced
description: Implement an automatic logoff mechanism to terminate user sessions after a period of inactivity.

title: Automatic user session termination enforced
description: User sessions must be automatically terminated all defined events outlined in the Compliance NIST 800-171 Policy. Conditions may include inactivity for a specified period (e.g., 15 minutes), time-of-day restrictions on access, and responses to suspicious activity or security incidents. Automatic session termination ensures system security by ending processes associated with the user's logical session.

title: Biometric signature uniqueness ensured
description: Design biometric‑based signatures so they cannot be used by anyone other than the genuine owner.

title: Changes to PHI logged
description: Track all changes made to PHI, including additions, modifications, and deletions, to ensure data integrity and enable audit trails.

title: Credential loss response executed
description: Follow loss‑management procedures to deauthorize lost/stolen/missing tokens or devices and issue replacements under rigorous controls.

title: Dedicated administrator accounts used
description: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts on enterprise assets. Conduct general computing activities, such as internet browsing, email, and productivity suite use, from the user’s primary, non-privileged account.

title: Default accounts disabled
description: Disable default accounts on enterprise assets and software, such as root, administrator, and other pre-configured vendor accounts, where possible. Example implementations can include: disabling default accounts or making them unusable.

title: Dormant accounts disabled
description: Monitor for dormant user accounts and disable or remove accounts that have been inactive for an extended period.

title: Electronic signature components managed
description: Require multi‑factor or multi-component e‑signature mechanisms for non‑biometric systems.

title: Emergency access procedure established
description: Establish and maintain a procedure for granting authorized personnel access to critical information during emergencies.

title: Employee access regularly reviewed
description: Employee access is reviewed at least annually to ensure that access privileges are appropriate and that former employees or users do not retain unauthorized access.

title: IAM Inventory Maintained
description: Maintain a centralized inventory of all individuals, groups, roles, services, and devices with access to organizational systems.

title: Identity management
description: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.

title: Information access restriction
description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.

title: Key inventory maintained
description: Maintain an inventory of all shared or production access tokens to ensure proper management, tracking, and security of our key assets.

title: MFA required for administrative access
description: Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for administrative access to critical systems and services. MFA mitigates the risk of unauthorized administrative access and helps protect sensitive administrative functions.

title: MFA required for applications
description: Require all externally-exposed enterprise or third-party applications to enforce MFA, where supported. Enforcing MFA through a directory service or SSO provider is a satisfactory implementation of this safeguard.

title: MFA required for critical services
description: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is required for accessing critical services and infrastructure unless a documented exception is in place where MFA is not supported.

title: MFA required for infrastructure access
description: Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for accessing sensitive infrastructure. MFA adds an extra layer of security by requiring users to provide additional authentication factors beyond their passwords.

title: MFA required for network access
description: Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for accessing the organization's network. MFA strengthens network security by requiring users to provide additional authentication factors during login attempts.

title: Maintenance control process established
description: Establish a process to approve and monitor maintenance and diagnostic activities requiring remote network access to manage or maintain systems—this encompasses all maintenance activities for applications hosted on a cloud provider.

title: Maintenance personnel authorization process established
description: Establish a process for authorizing maintenance personnel and organizations to ensure only qualified and authorized individuals perform maintenance on the system. Verify access authorizations for non-escorted personnel and designate authorized personnel to supervise maintenance activities when required.

title: Maintenance tools managed and secured
description: Implement a process to approve, control, and monitor the use of system maintenance tools, ensuring they are free from malicious code and do not compromise the security of CUI. Additionally, prevent unauthorized removal of system maintenance equipment containing CUI by sanitizing, retaining, or securely destroying such equipment as outlined in the Asset Management Policy.

title: Password expiration and renewal enforced
description: Periodically check, recall, or revise identification codes and passwords (e.g., password aging) to maintain security and integrity.

title: Password management policy enforced
description: Strictly enforce the organization’s password management policy to guarantee compliance with security standards. Enforcing this policy includes implementing technical controls, monitoring adherence, and responding to non-compliance.

title: Password management policy established
description: Enforce a password management policy that mandates strong and complex passwords, and prohibits the reuse of previously used passwords. This policy helps protect user accounts from unauthorized access due to weak or compromised passwords.

title: Secure authentication
description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.

title: Signature during continuous session captured
description: For a series of signings in a single, continuous, controlled session: require full credentials for the first signing; allow subsequent signings with at least one component (e.g., PIN) that only the individual can execute.

title: Signature outside continuous session captured
description: When signings are not in a single continuous session, require all electronic signature components for each signing.

title: Unique identification codes managed
description: Manage IDs/passwords securely with lifecycle controls to prevent misuse.

title: Unique signature identification assigned
description: Ensure each e‑signature is unique, non‑transferable, and tied to one individual.

title: Unique user identification implemented
description: Enforce the use of unique user identification for all individuals and systems accessing environments where protected information is stored, processed, or transmitted.

title: Use restricted to genuine owners
description: Ensure electronic signatures (non‑biometric) are used only by their genuine owners.

title: AI data governance established
description: Establish and maintain data governance processes for AI systems covering data acquisition, quality, provenance, preparation, and lifecycle management.

title: AI data lineage tracked
description: Track and document data lineage throughout the AI system lifecycle, including data origin, transformations, usage, and flow from source to AI model outputs.

title: AI data quality assessment performed
description: Assess and ensure the quality of data used for AI systems meets defined requirements for accuracy, completeness, consistency, and fitness for purpose.

title: AI model validation performed
description: Validate AI models to ensure they meet intended objectives, perform as expected in real-world conditions, and are fit for their intended purpose and use cases.

title: AI system development process established
description: Define and document processes for the responsible design, development, verification, validation, and deployment of AI systems throughout their lifecycle.

title: AI system testing performed
description: Perform comprehensive testing of AI systems to verify they meet specifications, function correctly, and are secure and reliable throughout their lifecycle.

title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
description: Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented and implemented.

title: Automated Request Handling Workflow
description: Establish an automated workflow for handling consumer privacy requests in compliance with the CCPA. The system captures, validates, and routes requests—such as access, deletion, or opt-out of sale—ensuring timely and accurate processing. Audit trails and reporting capabilities are maintained to demonstrate compliance and support ongoing monitoring and remediation.

title: Classification of information
description: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organization based on confidentiality, integrity, availability and relevant interested party requirements.

title: Clear Link to Not Share Information
description: This control ensures that the website homepage includes clear and accessible links titled “Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information” and “Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information” in compliance with the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and its amendments (CPRA). These links allow consumers to easily exercise their rights to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal data and to restrict the use of sensitive personal information. The control verifies that the links are prominently displayed, functional, and direct users to appropriate mechanisms or preference centers for submitting their requests.

title: Collect Only Necessary Data
description: Collect only the personal information necessary to fulfill its stated business purposes, in alignment with CCPA principles. Data minimization practices are applied across all collection points to limit exposure and reduce privacy risks. Regular reviews are conducted to ensure that unnecessary data is not captured or retained, supporting compliance and responsible data handling.

title: Compliance for multiple covered functions managed
description: Entities performing multiple covered functions must establish clear processes to ensure each function adheres to its specific standards and requirements.

title: Conduct Privacy Impact Assessments for New Processes
description: The organization must conduct privacy impact assessments (PIAs) for new processes being introduced to the data flow. Some example processes may be introducing a new plan for marketing emails, introducing a new payment processor, or introducing a new system for data analytics. This document should be approved by the privacy officer before action takes place.

title: Confidential customer communication channel created
description: Establish and maintain a secure communication channel for all customer interactions involving protected information.

title: Consent for collecting and managing data obtained
description: Implement a consent mechanism that is freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous. The consent requests must be clearly distinguishable from other matters, in an easily accessible form, using clear and simple language. For sensitive data, obtain explicit consent unless another exception applies.Maintain a record of obtained consents, including what the data subject has consented to and when.Regularly review and renew consents, especially if your purposes for processing change.Do not make services conditional on consent unless the data is necessary for that service.

title: Cookie And Tracker Management
description: This control requires the implementation of a Cookie and Tracker Management Platform (CMP) to manage user consent for cookies and tracking technologies in compliance with CCPA and related privacy regulations. The CMP collects, records, and enforces user preferences for data collection, sharing, and marketing purposes. It also provides mechanisms for users to review and update their consent, while maintaining audit logs to demonstrate regulatory compliance.

title: Criminal record data processing managed
description: If you process any data related to criminal convictions or offenses, verify that you have the legal authority to process this data.Implement strict access controls and security measures for this sensitive data. Document your legal basis and safeguards for processing criminal record data.Make sure you're not maintaining a comprehensive register of criminal convictions unless under official authority.

title: Cryptographic key lifecycle managed
description: Develop and document detailed key management policies and procedures covering the entire key lifecycle, including:- Implement secure key generation processes using strong cryptographic algorithms.- Establish secure methods for key distribution and storage.- Define and enforce key usage policies, including access controls and key rotation schedules.- Implement processes for secure key backup and recovery.- Establish procedures for key revocation, replacement, and destruction when necessary.- Implement split knowledge and dual control for manual cleartext key operations.- Prevent unauthorized substitution of cryptographic keys.- Require formal acknowledgment of responsibilities from key custodians.Regularly review and update key management practices to align with industry standards.

title: Cryptographic keys and application secrets secured
description: Implement and maintain robust procedures to securely manage cryptographic keys and application secrets used for protecting sensitive data throughout their lifecycle.Develop and document a comprehensive key management policy and procedures, including:- Implement secure key generation processes using industry-standard algorithms and random number generators.- Establish secure key storage mechanisms, such as hardware security modules (HSMs) or encrypted key stores.- Enforce strict access controls to limit key/secret access to authorized personnel only.- Implement key rotation and expiration policies to minimize the impact of potential key compromises.- Set up secure key/secret distribution and transmission processes.- Establish and maintain an inventory of all cryptographic keys and their purposes.- Implement secure key/secret backup and recovery procedures.- Ensure proper destruction of retired or compromised keys and secrets.Regularly review and update key management practices to align with current industry standards and best practices.

title: Data deletion enforced
description: Implement and maintain procedures to securely delete data when it is no longer needed, in compliance with data protection regulations and company policies.

title: Data encrypted at rest
description: All sensitive data is encrypted when stored on systems or devices.

title: Data encrypted in-transit
description: All data is encrypted when transmitted over networks, both within the organization's internal network and external connections.

title: Data inventory maintained
description: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all data assets. This can include data stored in databases, file shares, and cloud storage.

title: Data labeled according to classification level
description: All data is labeled based on its classification level. This ensures that appropriate access controls and handling procedures are applied to protect information.

title: Data leakage prevention
description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.

title: Data management and retention policy established
description: A data management and retention policy is established, outlining guidelines for how long data should be retained and how it should be managed throughout its lifecycle.

title: Data masking
description: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration.

title: Data masking procedures used
description: Implement data masking procedures to replace sensitive or personally identifiable information with fictitious but realistic data. Ensure data masking is applied consistently across all non-production environments, such as development, testing, and training systems, to protect the confidentiality of sensitive data.

title: Data processing integrity and output validated
description: Define and establish quality control measures to verify the completeness, accuracy, and timeliness of your output. This can look like implementing automated data validation checks, setting up data reconciliation processes, establishing review and approval workflows for critical outputs, creating data quality dashboards for real-time monitoring, and implementing version control systems for data processing scripts. Set up real-time monitoring of these measures and create error logs and incident reports where applicable.

title: Data retention policy enforced
description: The data retention policy is actively enforced, ensuring that data is retained for the appropriate duration and disposed of securely when it reaches the end of its retention period.

title: Data transfer mechanisms established
description: Establish proper data transfer mechanisms to protect personal data when it's transferred across borders or to third parties.Identify all instances where personal data is transferred outside your organization or to different jurisdictions. Assess the adequacy of data protection measures in recipient countries or organizations.Implement appropriate transfer mechanisms (e.g., standard contractual clauses, binding corporate rules) based on the nature of the transfer and the recipient.Establish processes to regularly review and update transfer mechanisms as regulatory requirements or organizational practices change. Maintain documentation of all data transfer arrangements and their legal bases.

title: De-identification process for PHI established
description: Establish and implement a documented process for de-identifying protected health information (PHI) to comply with HIPAA requirements and enable the use or disclosure of data without patient authorization if required.

title: Establish Right-To-Correct Request Handling
description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-correct management. Under CCPA, the user has a right to have their inaccurate data corrected upon request.

title: External privacy inquiries managed
description: Establish and maintain a system for handling all privacy-related inquiries, including general questions, complaints, disputes, and specific requests for personal information access and correction.

title: Facility directory disclosure process established
description: Establish and maintain a documented process for including protected health information (PHI) in facility directories, ensuring individuals are informed and given the opportunity to agree or object to such disclosures.

title: Full card numbers protected and hidden
description: Implement different measures to protect full card numbers (i.e. PANs) such as hiding most of the number on screens and receipts with something like asterisks (\*\*\*\*\*\*\*). Establish a list of job roles who need full access, including a rationale why this is the case.Set up your payment system so that it only shows full numbers to people on your access list. Be extra careful with remote access and set up controls to only let those with a real business need copy card numbers.Train your staff accordingly. Review access rights to full card numbers regularly and check your system configurations.

title: Honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) Signals
description: Detect and honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals from users’ browsers or devices in accordance with CCPA requirements. It automatically respects users’ preferences to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal information. The control also logs and monitors signal processing to maintain compliance and provide evidence of adherence to consumer privacy requests.

title: Information backup
description: Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.

title: Information deletion
description: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.

title: Information transfer
description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties.

title: Input validation used
description: Implement input validation checks across all system interfaces to ensure data completeness and accuracy before processing. These checks should verify that data meets specified format requirements and business rules.

title: Key management process implemented
description: Implement a key management system covering the entire lifecycle of cryptographic keys.

title: Labelling of information
description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization.

title: Maintain Record of Processing Activities
description: The organization must maintain a comprehensive and regularly updated record of all personal information processing activities to demonstrate compliance with CCPA.

title: Media accounted for and documented during transportation
description: Establish a process to protect and control system media containing CUI during transport outside of controlled areas. Ensure accountability through documented activities and tracking of media to prevent loss, tampering, or destruction.

title: Media physically secured
description: Implement physical security measures such as storing all media (like paper documents, laptops, USB drives) in locked cabinets and restricting access to only authorized personnel. Develop a system to classify all media based on the sensitivity of the data it contains. Maintain an inventory for all media, both electronic and physical, and conduct a full inventory check at least once every 12 months.Establish procedures for secure distribution (e.g., logging of all media sent outside the facility, using secure couriers). Obtain management approval before moving any media offsite.Create and log procedures for secure destruction (e.g., shredding paper, secure data wiping methods).Train employees at least yearly so that they can identify and manage this type of confidential data.

title: Notice of privacy practices created and distributed
description: Distribute a Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP) to patients, informing them of their rights under HIPAA and how their PHI will be used and disclosed.

title: PHI access and amendment requests managed
description: Establish and maintain a documented process to manage individuals' requests for accessing and amending their protected health information (PHI).

title: PHI disclosure records maintained
description: Establish a system to track disclosures of PHI, ensuring records of disclosures meet HIPAA requirements for accountability and transparency.

title: Patient authorization for PHI use and disclosure obtained
description: Require and maintain patient authorizations for the use or disclosure of PHI outside of permitted purposes (treatment, payment, and healthcare operations).

title: Privacy and protection of PII
description: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of PII according to applicable laws and regulations and contractual requirements.

title: Privacy disclosure and notification mechanisms established
description: Implement and maintain active communication channels for informing individuals about specific instances of personal data collection, use, and disclosure. The goal is to provide timely, context-specific privacy information beyond what's in the general privacy policy.Develop templates for data sharing, policy changes, and breach notifications so they can be sent to the relevant stakeholders when needed. This includes identifying relevant authorities, setting up systems to deliver these notices via appropriate channels (e.g., app, email), and establishing a process to handle responses to these notifications.Regularly review and update these mechanisms.

title: Privacy policy created and maintained
description: A privacy policy is developed, regularly updated, and made accessible to all relevant parties. The goal is to enable transparent communication of data handling practices and to protect individuals' privacy rights.

title: Processing integrity policy established
description: Establish systematic controls in your processing integrity policy that ensure the completeness, accuracy, validity, timeliness, and authorization of system processing while maintaining data quality throughout the entire processing lifecycle.

title: Record copies generated and export enabled
description: Enable generating complete and precise copies of electronic records in both human‑readable and electronic formats for regulatory examination.

title: Right to Delete Request Handling
description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-delete management. Under CCPA, the user has a right to have their data deleted upon request.

title: Right to Limit Use of Sensitive PI
description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-limit use of SPI. Under CCPA, the user has a right to have their sensitive data not be shared upon request.

title: Right to Opt out of Sale and Sharing
description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-Opt-out of sale. Under CCPA, the user has a right to have their data not be shared upon request.

title: Right-to-Know Request Handling
description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-know management. Under CCPA, the user has a right to know what data of theirs has been collected.

title: Role-Specific Privacy Training
description: This control ensures that all employees receive role-specific privacy training to understand their responsibilities under the CCPA and related privacy laws. The training covers the handling of personal information, consumer rights, and organizational policies for protecting data. Completion is tracked, and refresher sessions are provided periodically to maintain awareness and compliance across the workforce.

title: Secure disposal process established
description: Establish and maintain a documented procedure for the secure disposal of electronic and physical media containing protected or classified data. This process renders the data unusable, unreadable, and indecipherable to prevent unauthorized access.

title: Sensitive payment information automatically deleted
description: Implement a system that automatically deletes sensitive payment information after it's no longer needed for business or legal purposes.For the purposes of this control sensitive data is any of the following:- Full track data (all the info from a card's magnetic stripe)- Card verification code (the 3 or 4 digit code on the card)- PIN (Personal Identification Number) or PIN blockConfigure your payment system to automatically delete this sensitive data right after approval. If you must keep it temporarily and have a solid business justification, make sure it's encrypted and deleted as soon as possible.Implement regular checks and reviews to make sure that no sensitive data is being stored. Train your team and instruct them to not write down or store this sensitive data

title: Signature manifestation on human‑readable forms displayed
description: Ensure that the printed name, date/time of signing, and meaning of the signature appear and are controlled like the record itself on any display or printout of a signed electronic record.

title: Signature manifestation provided
description: Ensure electronic signatures clearly indicate signer identity, timestamp, and purpose on human‑readable outputs.

title: Signatures bound to records
description: Permanently link each signature to its record to prevent dissociation or fraud.

title: System use notification mechanisms established
description: Implement and maintain a system use notification message that includes privacy and security notices and must be displayed and acknowledged before granting access to any system accessing sensitive data.

title: Test information
description: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed.

title: Track Gaps and Remediation
description: This control ensures that any gaps identified in the privacy program—whether through audits, assessments, or regulatory reviews—are formally tracked and addressed. It requires the organization to document each gap, assign remediation actions, monitor progress, and verify completion to maintain compliance with CCPA and related privacy requirements. The control supports continuous improvement by ensuring that privacy risks are systematically mitigated and accountability is maintained.

title: Travel to high-risk locations secured
description: Establish more stringent configurations for systems or components issued to employees traveling to high-risk areas. Upon return, conduct post-travel inspections of devices.

title: Unintended PHI contained and deleted
description: Establish a process to contain and securely delete any Personal Health Information (PHI) added accidentally by your users.

title: Unintended PHI detected
description: Implement mechanisms to detect PHI in your systems in case it accidentally gets added by your users, with an escalation process when potential PHI is detected.

title: Unintended PHI exposure reviewed
description: Establish a regular process to review all instances of unintended Personal Health Information (PHI) exposure to understand the root cause, assess impact, and determine whether any changes are needed to prevent future exposures.

title: Use and disclosure of PHI managed
description: Implement procedures to operationalize the policy defining the permissible uses and disclosures of protected health information (PHI), ensuring compliance with HIPAA requirements in day-to-day activities.

title: Verification of Consumer Identity
description: Verify the identity of consumers submitting privacy requests in accordance with CCPA requirements. Verification procedures ensure that access, deletion, or opt-out requests are processed only for legitimate requestors, protecting personal information from unauthorized disclosure. The process is documented, monitored, and updated as needed to maintain security and regulatory compliance.

title: Verification procedures established
description: Establish and maintain documented procedures for verifying the identity and authority of individuals or entities requesting access to protected information.

title: Workflow sequencing and process controls enforced
description: Ensure processes enforce correct sequence of operations and detect invalid transitions.

title: Automated backups enabled
description: Automated backups are enabled for all high-risk data and critical systems.

title: Business continuity and disaster recovery plan documented
description: Document clear and practical business continuity and disaster recovery plan that outline procedures for maintaining essential operations during disruptions.

title: Business continuity and disaster recovery policy established
description: A comprehensive business continuity and disaster recovery policy is established, outlining the organization's strategies for responding to disruptive incidents and supporting business continuity.

title: Business continuity plans documented
description: Document clear and practical business continuity plans that outline procedures for maintaining essential operations during disruptions.

title: Business continuity plans tested
description: Regularly test your business continuity plans to confirm their effectiveness and readiness to maintain critical operations during disruptions.

title: Business impact analysis performed
description: To identify and prioritize critical business functions, assess potential impacts of disruptions, and determine recovery strategies through systematic business impact analysis (BIA).

title: Crisis plan prepared
description: Develop a crisis plan to facilitate a coordinated and effective response to security or operational incidents that may impact critical systems.

title: Crisis plan tested
description: Regularly test your crisis plan to validate its effectiveness and ensure all team members are prepared to respond swiftly and efficiently to potential disruptions. Define a testing schedule based on your organization’s risk profile and regulatory requirements. Conduct tests at least annually, or more frequently for high-risk scenarios, to promote ongoing preparedness.You can use a variety of testing methods, such as tabletop exercises, live simulations, and role-playing scenarios. Include all necessary stakeholders in testing exercises.Record outcomes from each test, noting any gaps, response delays, or areas requiring improvement. Document lessons learned, recommended changes, and action items to refine the crisis plan based on test results.

title: Data backup and recovery policy established
description: Develop and maintain a backup and recovery policy that defines the your approach to protecting and preserving critical data.

title: Data recovery plan established
description: Establish a data recovery plan that defines procedures for recovering data in case of data loss, corruption, or system failures. A robust data recovery plan helps minimize downtime and data loss in critical situations.

title: Data recovery process established
description: Establish a data recovery process that defines procedures for recovering data in case of data loss, corruption, or system failures. A robust data recovery process helps minimize downtime and data loss in critical situations.

title: Data recovery tested
description: Regularly test the data recovery process to validate its effectiveness and ensure that data can be successfully restored from backups. Testing helps identify any issues or gaps in the data recovery plan.

title: Digital operational resilience strategy created
description: Establish and maintain a digital operational resilience strategy that outlines how your organization will ensure continuity, reliability, and security of critical ICT systems. This strategy should align with your business objectives and comply with regulatory standards focusing on preparedness, response, recovery, and adaptation to ICT-related disruptions.

title: Disaster recovery plans documented
description: Document disaster recovery (DR) plans to recover critical systems and maintain operational continuity following disruptive incidents. These plans should address potential risks, define recovery strategies, and integrate with broader business resilience efforts.

title: Disaster recovery plans tested
description: Regularly test the organization's disaster recovery plans to ensure their effectiveness and identify areas for improvement. Testing helps validate the ability to recover critical systems and operations in the event of a disaster.

title: Recovery data isolated
description: Isolate the recovery data from the production environment to prevent accidental overwriting or corruption of backups. Keeping recovery data separate helps maintain the integrity and availability of backup copies.

title: DMARC policy and verification used
description: DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance) policy and verification mechanisms are implemented to prevent email spoofing and phishing attacks.

title: Email account access restricted
description: Access to email accounts is restricted to administrators only, and isn't delegated to other non-admin users within the organization.

title: Email settings block malicious content
description: Email settings are configured to block malicious content, including malicious attachments, links, and scripts.

title: Anti-malware deployed on end-user devices
description: Anti-malware or antivirus solutions are deployed on end-user devices, such as laptops and workstations.

title: Application packages automatically updated
description: Utilize a library or package dependency management tool which automatically updates dependencies with known vulnerabilities.

title: Automatic session locking enforced
description: Enforce automatic session locking to prevent unauthorized access to unattended devices. Automatic session locking helps protect sensitive data from unauthorized access and misuse.

title: Cabling security
description: Cables carrying power, data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference or damage.

title: Clear desk and clear screen
description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.

title: Configuration defaults established
description: Define, document, and enforce configuration defaults for systems that align with the most restrictive mode consistent with operational requirements. Identify, document, and approve deviations from these settings to maintain a secure and controlled system environment.

title: Data encrypted on end-user devices
description: Data stored on end-user devices (e.g., laptops, mobile devices) is encrypted to protect it in case of device loss or theft.

title: End user devices monitored for compliance
description: Deploy a device compliance monitoring solution to track and verify the security status of end-user devices. This allows organizations to ensure devices meet security requirements without implementing full mobile device management.

title: Endpoint detection and response deployed
description: Implement Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) tools to monitor endpoints for suspicious activity, unauthorized changes, and vulnerabilities. EDR solutions should be configured to provide real-time alerts for potential threats, enabling timely investigation and response.

title: Equipment maintenance
description: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity and confidentiality of information.

title: Equipment siting and protection
description: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected.

title: Firewall maintained on end-user devices
description: Firewalls are installed and properly maintained on end-user devices, such as laptops and workstations.

title: Installation of software on operational systems
description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems.

title: Mobile device management (MDM) used
description: A mobile device management (MDM) solution is used to manage and secure end-user devices.

title: Point-of-Interaction devices managed
description: Implement procedures to securely manage all Point-of-Interaction (POI) devices (e.g., card payment devices) throughout their lifecycle, from deployment to decommissioning.To keep your payment devices safe:- Know your devices: Make an inventory of all your payment devices, including where they are located. Review this list every few months to make sure it's up to date.- Lock them down: Attach devices securely to counters so they can't be easily stolen and put tamper-evident stickers on device openings.- Check devices regularly: Look at each device daily for anything odd (like new attachments). Tell your staff to report anything suspicious right away.- Control who touches the devices: Only let authorized people install or fix devices. Always watch repair technicians when they work on devices.- Use strong passwords: Change the default password on every device and use unique, hard-to-guess passwords.- Train your team: Show everyone how to spot a tampered device and train them what to do if they see something suspicious.- Be careful when getting rid of old devices: Wipe all data from devices before throwing them away. Update your inventory accordingly.

title: Privileged access rights
description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

title: Return of assets
description: Personnel and other interested parties as appropriate shall return all the organization's assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

title: Secure device disposal process used
description: Implement and follow procedures for securely disposing of devices that contain organizational data. This ensures that sensitive information cannot be recovered from retired, damaged, or replaced devices.

title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
description: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

title: Security of assets off-premises
description: Off-site assets shall be protected.

title: Software inventory for applications maintained
description: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all licensed software installed for applications. This can be a link to a package or manifest file.

title: Software inventory on end-user devices maintained
description: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all licensed software installed on enterprise assets, including end-user devices and servers.

title: Storage media controls
description: Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition, use, transportation and disposal in accordance with the organization's classification scheme and handling requirements.

title: Supporting utilities
description: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.

title: Unauthorized software on end-user devices addressed and removed
description: Ensure that unauthorized software is either removed from use on enterprise assets or receives a documented exception. Review monthly, or more frequently based on risk level, to ensure software compliance with the organizational policy.

title: Unexpected file changes monitored
description: Set up a system to monitor unexpected changes to your important files and set up alerts when they happen.Create a list of your most important files. This should include things like system files, application files, configuration files, and log files. Review and update this list on a regular basis.Set up a file monitoring tool or intrusion detection tool with file monitoring capabilities. It should check all the important files on your list at least once a week. Configure it to send alerts immediately if it spots any unexpected changes. This could be changes to the file's content, or if files are added or deleted when they shouldn't be.Decide who on your team should receive these alerts and make sure they know what to do when they get one. Write down a clear procedure for how to investigate and respond to unexpected file changes. Keep records of all the alerts you receive and how you responded to them.Finally, check your file monitoring system at least once a month to make sure it's working as intended.

title: Unnecessary wireless capabilities disabled
description: Wireless networking capabilities that are not required for essential business or mission functions must be disabled on all devices to minimize security risks. Necessary wireless features, such as Wi-Fi or Bluetooth, must be configured securely to prevent unauthorized access.

title: User endpoint devices
description: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

title: Web filtering
description: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

title: Wireless access points identified and monitored
description: Test for the presence of all Wi-Fi access points at least once every three months and use tools or methods that can detect both authorized and unauthorized access points. If possible, use automated monitoring tools that can alert personnel when new access points are detected.Maintain an up-to-date list of all authorized wireless access points and document a business justification for each authorized access point. Regularly review and update this inventory.

title: Wireless environments configured
description: Set up and manage your wireless network securely for both office and remote workers. Change default settings such as:- Change all default passwords on your wireless equipment (routers, access points).- Replace any pre-set encryption keys.- Update SNMP community strings if you use them.- Check your device manuals for any other default settings and change those too.Enable the strongest encryption your devices support (preferably WPA3 or at least WPA2) and use a long, complex password for the Wi-Fi network. Change it regularly and control access (e.g., use a separate guest network for visitors if possible).Regularly check for unauthorized devices on your network and train your employees accordingly.

title: Active discovery tools used
description: An active discovery tool is used to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network, configured to execute daily or more frequently.

title: Administrator access restricted
description: Restrict administrator access to critical systems and sensitive data based on the principle of least privilege, granting elevated permissions only when necessary for specific tasks and revoking them promptly after completion. Implement strong authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication, and regularly review and update administrator access rights to ensure they align with job responsibilities and maintain a secure environment.

title: Anti-malware deployed on infrastructure
description: Deploy anti-malware or antivirus solutions on infrastructure components such as servers and network devices. This helps detect and prevent malware infections and other malicious activities targeting critical systems.

title: Application changes reviewed
description: All proposed application changes are reviewed before they impact production.

title: Automated security scanning performed on infrastructure
description: Automated security scanning software is deployed on all infrastructure components including servers and network devices.

title: Automatic network connection termination
description: Network connections associated with communication sessions must be automatically terminated at the end of the session or after a period of inactivity as defined in the Network Security Policy. This ensures that unused connections are not left open, reducing the risk of unauthorized access or misuse.

title: Buckets not exposed publicly
description: Cloud storage buckets are not exposed to the public internet unless a documented business justification is in place.

title: Clock synchronization
description: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organization shall be synchronized to approved time sources.

title: Clock synchronization enforced
description: Enforce clock synchronization across all systems and devices within the organization's network using a reliable and accurate time source, such as Network Time Protocol (NTP) or Precision Time Protocol (PTP). Regularly monitor and audit system clocks to ensure they remain synchronized and promptly address any discrepancies to maintain the integrity of time-dependent processes, such as logging, authentication, and transaction processing.

title: Cloud infrastructure used
description: Cloud infrastructure is hosted with providers that maintain independent certifications (e.g., SOC 2) for physical and environmental security controls, rather than managed on-premises.

title: Configuration management
description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.

title: Configuration management system established
description: Implement a configuration management system to manage and control the configuration of systems, applications, and infrastructure. Configuration management helps maintain consistency and security across the IT environment.

title: DNS filtering used
description: Utilize a DNS filtering service to protect against malicious domain requests and prevent access to known malicious websites.

title: Firewall restricts public access to infrastructure
description: Firewalls are configured to restrict public access to the organization's infrastructure components.

title: High availability infrastructure used
description: Deploy high availability infrastructure across multiple availability zones or regions to ensure system redundancy and fault tolerance. Configure load balancing, automatic failover, and data replication mechanisms to minimize downtime and maintain uninterrupted service in the event of hardware, software, or network failures.

title: Information flow control policy established
description: Establish an information flow control policy to regulate the secure transmission of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) within and between systems, enforcing encryption, access controls, and boundary protections.

title: Information security for use of cloud services
description: Processes for acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements.

title: Infrastructure changes logged
description: Maintain a log of all infrastructure changes to track and document modifications made to critical systems and services. Logging infrastructure changes aids in audit trails, incident investigations, and accountability.

title: Infrastructure changes require review
description: Implement a review process for all proposed infrastructure changes before implementation. Reviews ensure that changes comply with security policies, do not introduce vulnerabilities, and align with the organization's requirements.

title: Infrastructure deployed using an infrastructure-as-code tool
description: Adopt an infrastructure-as-code (IaC) approach to deploy and manage the organization's infrastructure components. IaC tools enable consistent and version-controlled infrastructure deployment, reducing the risk of configuration errors.

title: Intrusion detection system used
description: Utilize an intrusion detection system (IDS) to monitor network traffic and detect suspicious or unauthorized activities. IDS helps identify potential security breaches and intrusions in real-time.

title: Key management policy established
description: Establish a comprehensive key management policy that defines the processes for generating, storing, distributing, rotating, and revoking cryptographic keys used for data encryption, digital signatures, and secure communication. Ensure the key management policy aligns with industry best practices and regulatory requirements, and regularly review and update the policy to maintain its effectiveness in protecting the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of sensitive information.

title: Logging performed
description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.

title: NIST 800-171 compliance policy established
description: Establish a compliance policy to govern access control, account authorization, data management, infrastructure security, and endpoint protection to ensure secure handling and protection of CUI.

title: Network infrastructure continuously updated
description: Ensure the network infrastructure components (e.g., routers, switches, firewalls) are continuously updated with the latest security patches and firmware updates. Regular updates help address known vulnerabilities and enhance network security.

title: Network security policy established
description: Develop and implement a network security policy that provides clear guidelines and requirements for securing your network infrastructure.

title: Networks security
description: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

title: Payment page scripts managed
description: All scripts loaded and executed on payment pages in the consumer's browser must be authorized, integrity-assured, and inventoried with justification.Implement a method to authorize each payment page script and establish a process to confirm the integrity of each script. Maintain an inventory of all scripts with written justification for their necessity.Regularly review and update policies and procedures for managing payment page scripts.

title: Production and test environments separated
description: Maintain separate, dedicated environments for testing and production systems, ensuring changes are thoroughly evaluated before implementation into the live production environment.

title: Production deployment access restricted
description: Limit access to production deployment environments to authorized personnel only. This control helps prevent unauthorized changes or deployments that may disrupt critical services.

title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.

title: Pull requests used
description: Pull requests are used for code changes to ensure all modifications are reviewed before merging to production branches.

title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

title: Security of network services
description: Security mechanisms, service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.

title: Segregation of networks
description: Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated in the organization's networks.

title: Systems and software kept up to date
description: Automate and standardize the process of deploying software patches and updates to systems, software, and applications to ensure that critical security patches are applied promptly to address known vulnerabilities.

title: Systems segregated according to sensitivity of business function
description: Set up your systems so that different parts of your business operations are kept separate or well-protected from each other, especially when they have different security needs.In this context, a "function" is a specific business operation or process that your IT systems handle. For example:- Public website hosting- Employee email system- Customer database management- Payment processingIdentify your business operations and list out the key tasks your IT systems complete. Evaluate which functions need higher security and which need less. Separate them where possible (e.g., use different servers or cloud services for operations with different security needs). If you can't physically separate use virtualization to create separate environments on the same machine.Secure everything to the level of the most sensitive function.Document your setup by creating a diagram showing which business operations are on which systems. Explain how you've separated or secured different operations.

title: Unauthorized assets addressed and removed
description: Ensure that a process exists to address unauthorized assets on a periodic basis. This process should include regular audits of all assets and a procedure for handling unauthorized assets when they are discovered.

title: Unique production database authentication enforced
description: Enforce unique authentication mechanisms for accessing production databases, such as a unique username and password or SSH key.

title: Use of cryptography
description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

title: VPN used
description: Utilize Virtual Private Network (VPN) connections to secure remote communications between users and the organization's internal network. VPNs provide encrypted and authenticated tunnels, ensuring secure data transmission over untrusted networks.

title: Web Application Firewall (WAF) used
description: Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to protect web applications from various cyber threats, such as SQL injection, cross-site scripting, and other application-layer attacks.

title: AI system monitoring established
description: Establish ongoing monitoring of AI systems to track performance, detect issues, identify drift, and ensure continued safe and effective operation in production.

title: Adequate audit log storage maintained
description: Ensure sufficient storage capacity to retain audit logs for the required duration. Adequate audit log storage enables historical analysis and supports compliance requirements related to log retention.

title: Assessment and decision on information security events
description: The organization shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorized as information security incidents.

title: Audit log management process maintained
description: Maintain a robust and up-to-date audit log management process. This process should include guidelines for capturing, storing, and monitoring audit logs, ensuring the availability and integrity of essential security event data.

title: Audit logs collected
description: Enable the collection of audit logs from critical systems and applications. Audit logs capture essential security events and activities, providing valuable information for incident detection, investigation, and compliance purposes.

title: Breach notification process established
description: Establish a breach notification process that outlines the organization's procedures for detecting, assessing, and reporting security breaches. This process helps ensure timely and effective incident response.

title: Breach notification template prepared
description: Develop and maintain a standardized breach notification template to provide timely, consistent, and compliant communication with affected individuals in the event of a breach.

title: Breach response playbook established
description: Develop and maintain a breach response playbook focused on managing breaches after they are discovered, including detailed procedures for assessing, containing, and resolving incidents involving protected information.

title: Breach risk assessment template developed
description: Establish a standardized risk assessment template to evaluate the potential impact of breaches on affected individuals. Include criteria for assessing:- The nature and sensitivity of the breached ePHI.- The likelihood of data misuse.- Steps already taken to mitigate the breach.

title: Compliance monitoring processes defined and implemented
description: Define and implement comprehensive compliance monitoring processes to evaluate the performance and effectiveness of the information security management system (ISMS).

title: Critical security control systems monitored
description: Your security tools are like the alarm system for your business. If they stop working and you don't notice, you're leaving the door wide open for attackers. By catching and fixing problems fast, you keep your defenses strong and your customer data safe.Make a list of your key security tools, including:- Firewalls and other network security controls- Intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS)- Anti-malware software- Systems that detect changes to important files- Physical access controls (like door locks)- Login systems- Tools that record system activities (audit logs)- Any tools that separate different parts of your network (segmentation controls)Configure each system to send out an alert if it stops working properly and make sure these alerts go to the right people who can fix the problem. Include this in the incident response plan for each type of security system.Regularly test your alert system to make sure it's working and maintain records for each security system failure.

title: Digital operational resilience testing program defined
description: Define a comprehensive testing program to evaluate your digital operational resilience across ICT systems and critical functions. Ensure the scope of the resilience testing program includes all critical ICT assets, systems, and processes. Set objectives for testing key resilience aspects such as incident response, disaster recovery, and business continuity.Determine appropriate testing methods, including tabletop exercises, penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and full-scale simulations. Schedule tests regularly, ensuring they reflect the criticality and risk profile of each asset or service.Analyze testing outcomes to identify areas for improvement and update security and resilience controls accordingly. Document action plans to address any identified gaps and implement corrective actions.

title: HHS breach reporting template established
description: Prepare a standardized reporting template for notifying the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) of breaches, including required elements for both small and large breaches.

title: ICT readiness for business continuity
description: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.

title: Incident history maintained
description: Maintain a record of how past incidents were handled, focusing on their identification, reporting, escalation, and resolution.

title: Incident manager assigned
description: Designate an incident manager who is responsible for overseeing the organization's incident response process. The incident manager coordinates the response efforts and ensures that incidents are handled effectively.

title: Incident response exercises performed
description: Conduct regular incident response exercises to test the organization's response capabilities and identify areas for improvement. Incident response exercises can include tabletop exercises, simulations, or red teaming activities.

title: Incident response policy established
description: An incident response policy is established that outlines the organization's approach and procedures for detecting, responding to, and recovering from cybersecurity incidents.

title: Incident response procedures defined
description: Create step-by-step response procedures covering incident detection, containment, eradication, and recovery. Include guidelines for different types of incidents, such as data breaches, malware infections, and system outages. Clearly designate roles within the incident response team, including incident handlers, communication leads, and recovery personnel.Also outline communication protocols for both internal and external stakeholders, specifying approved channels for incident notifications and updates.Regularly review and update incident response procedures.

title: Incident review process implemented
description: Establish a structured process for conducting incident reviews following any security or operational incident affecting critical systems. This process is essential for understanding root causes, assessing the impact, and identifying improvement actions to prevent similar incidents in the future. Establish criteria for incidents requiring formal review, including any events affecting critical functions. Define the scope to include root cause analysis, impact assessment, response effectiveness, and corrective actions.For each qualifying incident, hold a review meeting promptly after resolution. Ensure that relevant stakeholders participate. Record detailed findings from each review, including root causes, identified vulnerabilities, and gaps in response. Document action items, timelines, and responsible parties for implementing corrective measures to prevent recurrence.Establish a follow-up process to verify that corrective actions are completed and effective.

title: Information security during disruption
description: The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.

title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
description: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing and communicating information security incident management processes, roles and responsibilities.

title: Infrastructure performance monitored
description: The performance of the organization's infrastructure components is monitored to detect potential issues or anomalies that may impact security or reliability.

title: Learning from information security incidents
description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.

title: Log management used
description: Implement a centralized log management solution to collect, store, and analyze logs from various systems and applications. Centralized log management simplifies log review, correlation, and monitoring for potential security incidents.

title: Logging and monitoring policy established
description: Establish comprehensive system logging and monitoring requirements that enable detection of security events, support incident investigations, and maintain operational visibility across information systems.

title: Media notification template established
description: Define and maintain standardized templates for media-related notifications to ensure consistent handling and documentation of security events

title: Monitoring activities
description: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

title: Monitoring procedure defined
description: Define a clear monitoring procedure to track the performance of critical systems and processes.

title: Monitoring supplier dependencies
description: The organization shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.

title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation
description: The organization shall determine: a) what needs to be monitored and measured, including information security processes and controls; b) the methods for monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation, as applicable, to ensure valid results. The methods selected should produce comparable and reproducible results to be considered valid; c) when the monitoring and measuring shall be performed; d) who shall monitor and measure; e) when the results from monitoring and measurement shall be analyzed and evaluated; f) who shall analyze and evaluate these results. Documented information shall be available as evidence of the results. The organization shall evaluate the information security performance and the effectiveness of the information security management system.

title: Network infrastructure monitored
description: Implement monitoring mechanisms for the network infrastructure to detect and respond to suspicious or unauthorized activities. Network monitoring helps ensure the integrity and availability of network resources.

title: Processing activities monitored
description: Monitor system processing activities to detect and address processing errors, delays, or unauthorized modifications. This monitoring should include automated alerts for processing failures or anomalies.

title: Response to information security incidents
description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

title: Threat Intelligence
description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analyzed to produce threat intelligence.

title: Threat intelligence established
description: Create and maintain a threat intelligence program to gather, analyze, and share information about potential threats to your information and systems.

title: Transaction security and authentication safeguarded
description: Use transaction safeguards to prevent unauthorized use of IDs/passwords and detect and report attempted unauthorized use immediately to security and management, as appropriate.

title: Unauthorized changes to payment pages managed
description: Implement a change and tamper detection mechanism for your payment pages. This system should monitor two key elements: the HTTP headers (which contain metadata about the web page) and the actual content of the page, with particular attention to any scripts running on it. Configure your detection mechanism to identify any unauthorized modifications, additions, or deletions to these components of your payment pages.Determine the frequency of your page checks. You have two options:- Perform checks at least once a week (recommended for most businesses), or- Establish your own schedule based on a thorough risk analysis (if you're unsure about this process, weekly checks are advisable).Your detection system should promptly alert your team to any suspicious activity. Designate responsible personnel to receive these alerts and establish clear procedures for how to respond.If you're utilizing a payment Vendor that embeds their payment form on your website, request evidence that they're performing these checks on their end. They should be able to demonstrate compliance with this requirement.Regularly review and update your detection mechanism. As your payment pages evolve or new threats emerge, you may need to adjust your monitoring parameters.Maintain detailed records of all alerts received and your responses to them. This documentation aids in incident analysis and demonstrates your vigilance to auditors.

title: AI human oversight established
description: Ensure AI systems are appropriately overseen by competent individuals who can intervene, override decisions, and maintain human control over critical functions.

title: AI management system established
description: Establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve an AI management system (AIMS) that governs the responsible development, deployment, and use of AI systems throughout their lifecycle.

title: AI policy established
description: Establish and maintain an AI policy that defines the organization's approach to responsible development, deployment, and use of artificial intelligence systems.

title: AI system documentation maintained
description: Maintain comprehensive technical documentation for AI systems, including architecture, capabilities, limitations, and information for users and stakeholders.

title: Acceptable use policy established
description: Establish and maintain an acceptable use policy that outlines permissible activities, systems, and data access for all users, contractors, and third parties interacting with the organization's information assets and technologies.

title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.

title: Application security requirements
description: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.

title: Asset inventory maintained
description: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data. This can include end-user devices, network devices, IoT devices, and servers.

title: Asset management policy acknowledged by employees
description: All employees have acknowledged and agreed to the asset management policy. This policy outlines the guidelines for managing the organization's assets throughout their lifecycle.

title: Asset management policy established
description: Establish an asset management policy that outlines the guidelines for managing the organization's assets throughout their lifecycle.

title: Board charter documented
description: Document the Board charter, which outlines the responsibilities, roles, and authority of the Board of Directors. The charter sets the framework for effective corporate governance and oversight.

title: Board expertise developed
description: Ensure that the Board of Directors possesses the expertise and skills necessary to provide effective oversight and guidance on various business and industry matters.

title: Board meetings conducted
description: Conduct regular Board meetings to review organizational performance, strategic direction, and major decisions. Board meetings provide a platform for informed decision-making and governance.

title: Board oversight briefings conducted
description: Conduct periodic briefings for the Board of Directors to provide updates on cybersecurity and risk management matters. Board oversight briefings enable the Board to stay informed about the organization's security posture.

title: Business associate agreements managed
description: Establish and maintain a process for managing Business Associate Agreements (BAAs) to meet HIPAA requirements.

title: Business associate agreements with subcontractors managed
description: As a business associate, establish a process for managing contracts with subcontractors to ensure they comply with HIPAA requirements. These agreements must extend your obligations under BAAs with covered entities or upstream business associates, including safeguarding PHI, reporting breaches, and adhering to HIPAA standards.

title: Capacity management
description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.

title: Change management
description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.

title: Change management policy established
description: Establish a change management policy that defines procedures for controlling and documenting changes to systems, applications, and infrastructure.

title: Change management process established
description: Establish a formal change management policy that outlines procedures for reviewing, approving, implementing, and documenting changes to systems, software, and procedures. This policy ensures that all changes are controlled and do not negatively impact service delivery.

title: Changelog established and maintained
description: Establish and maintain a changelog to document all system changes, updates, and modifications.

title: Code of conduct enforced
description: Enforce the organization's code of conduct consistently and apply appropriatedisciplinary actions for violations. Enforcement helps maintain a positive workenvironment and fosters a culture of integrity.

title: Code of conduct established
description: A code of conduct is established that outlines the expected behavior and ethical standards for all employees.
