title: Code of conduct for contributors present in repository and enforced
description: Include and enforce a code of conduct for contributors in the organization's code repositories and open-source projects. The code of conduct sets guidelines for respectful and inclusive contributions to the community.

title: Collection of evidence
description: The organization shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events.

title: Communication Practices
description: The organization shall determine the need for internal and external communications relevant to the information security management system including: a) on what to communicate; b) when to communicate; c) with whom to communicate; d) how to communicate.

title: Communication procedure established
description: Establish and maintain a structured approach for internal and external communications related to your security program.

title: Company security commitments externally communicated
description: Key company security commitments and policies are externally communicated, including the Master Service Agreement (MSA), Security Information page, or Terms of Service.

title: Competence Measurement
description: The organization shall: a) determine the necessary competence of person(s) doing work under its control that affects its information security performance; b) ensure that these persons are competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, or experience; c) where applicable, take actions to acquire the necessary competence, and evaluate the effectiveness of the actions taken; and d) retain appropriate documented information as evidence of competence.

title: Complaint process established
description: Create and maintain a process to receive, investigate, and resolve complaints related to privacy violations.

title: Compliance program established and maintained
description: To manage your compliance program, start by getting your top executives involved. Assign a senior leader to be officially responsible for compliance and create a clear, written plan for how your company will stay compliant. Make sure all company leaders know about this plan.Every three months, someone (who isn't checking their own work) reviews that everyone is following security rules. These reviews should cover daily security log checks, network security settings, how new systems are set up securely, how you handle security alerts, and your change management processes. Keep detailed notes of these quarterly checks, writing down what's working well and what needs fixing. Make plans to address any issues you find. Have your compliance manager review and sign off on these results and any fix-it plans.Through all of this, make sure everyone knows their role in staying compliant and up to date on important security practices.

title: Compliance with policies
description: Compliance with the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed.

title: Confidentiality Agreement acknowledged by contractors
description: Require all contractors and third-party vendors to acknowledge and sign a confidentiality agreement. This agreement ensures that sensitive information shared with external parties is protected and not disclosed to unauthorized individuals.

title: Confidentiality Agreement acknowledged by employees
description: All employees have acknowledged and signed a confidentiality agreement. This agreement reinforces the commitment to safeguarding sensitive information and trade secrets.

title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organization's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.

title: Contact with authorities
description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities.

title: Contact with special interest groups
description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.

title: Continual Improvement
description: The organization shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the information security management system.

title: Continuous feedback process established
description: Maintain an ongoing feedback process where managers and employees engage in regular discussions about performance, goals, and development. This approach allows for timely adjustments to performance and immediate recognition of achievements.

title: Continuous professional development plan established
description: The organization is committed to developing and maintaining the competence of its employees by supporting continuous professional development. This includes encouraging employees to participate in relevant training, certifications, and skill-building activities to stay current with industry standards and align their skills with the organization's objectives.

title: Control of documented information
description: Documented information required by the information security management system and by this document shall be controlled to ensure: a) it is available and suitable for use, where and when it is needed; and b) it is adequately protected (e.g. from loss of confidentiality, improper use, or loss of integrity). For the control of documented information, the organization shall address the following activities, as applicable: c) distribution, access, retrieval and use; d) storage and preservation, including the preservation of legibility; e) control of changes (e.g. version control); and f) retention and disposition. Documented information of external origin, determined by the organization to be necessary for the planning and operation of the information security management system, shall be identified as appropriate, and controlled.

title: Creating and Updating Documentation
description: When creating and updating documented information the organization shall ensure appropriate: a) identification and description (e.g. a title, date, author, or reference number); b) format (e.g. language, software version, graphics) and media (e.g. paper, electronic); and c) review and approval for suitability and adequacy.

title: Data-flow diagrams maintained
description: Up-to-date data-flow diagram(s) are maintained that show all account data flows across systems and networks, updated as needed when changes occur in the environment.

title: Determining the scope of the information security management system
description: The organization shall determine the boundaries and applicability of the information security management system to establish its scope. When determining this scope, the organization shall consider: a) the external and internal issues referred to in 4.1; b) the requirements referred to in 4.2; c) interfaces and dependencies between activities performed by the organization, and those that are performed by other organizations. The scope shall be available as documented information.

title: Disciplinary process
description: A disciplinary process shall be formalized and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation

title: Documentation procedure established
description: Establish and maintain a structured approach for controlling documented information related to your security program to ensure availability, integrity, and protection.

title: Documented information
description: The organization’s information security management system shall include: a) documented information required by this document; and b) documented information determined by the organization as being necessary for the effectiveness of the information security management system.

title: Documented operating procedures
description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.

title: Electronic signature accountability enforced
description: Establish and enforce written policies that hold individuals accountable for all actions initiated under their electronic signatures to deter record/signature falsification.

title: Employee background checks performed
description: Conduct thorough background checks on potential employees to verify their identity, employment history, education, and criminal record. Background checks help ensure the suitability and integrity of candidates.

title: Employee screening
description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis taking into consideration applicable laws, regulations and ethics and be proportional to the business requirements, the classification of the information to be accessed and the perceived risks.

title: Employees trained on CUI handling
description: Establish a security training program to ensure all personnel are equipped to protect CUI, and appropriately manage public-facing content. The program must emphasize preventing unauthorized disclosure of CUI.

title: Employment contracts used
description: Ensure that you are using employment contracts for all of your employees. These contracts should include confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements, legal responsibilities and rights, responsibilities for data handling, and actions to be taken if employees disregard the organization's security requirements.

title: External support resources available (i.e., documentation)
description: Provide external support resources, such as documentation, user guides, and knowledge bases, to assist users in utilizing the organization's services effectively. Accessible support resources promote self-service and reduce support requests.

title: Group health plan managed
description: Establish and maintain a process to manage group health plans in compliance with HIPAA.

title: HIPAA training conducted
description: Establish a process for providing HIPAA training to workforce members to build understanding of their responsibilities related to protecting protected health information (PHI) and complying with HIPAA regulations.

title: Healthcare clearinghouse functions isolated
description: Establish a process to separate health care clearinghouse functions from other organizational operations to prevent unauthorized access or commingling of sensitive information.

title: Human resource security policy established
description: Develop and maintain a human resource policy that addresses information security responsibilities throughout the employment lifecycle, from recruitment to termination.

title: ISMS budget provided
description: Allocate a dedicated budget for the Information Security Management System (ISMS) to support the resources, tools, and personnel required to implement, maintain, and improve the organization’s security posture.

title: Identity verified before signature assignment
description: Verify a person's identity before establishing or sanctioning any element of their electronic signature.

title: Independent review of information security
description: The organization's approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals, or when significant changes occur.

title: Information security awareness
description: Personnel of the organization and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training and regular updates of the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

title: Information security communities identified
description: Stay connected with the broader security community by engaging with professional groups, industry forums, and security events to keep current with evolving threats and best practices.

title: Information security event reporting
description: The organization shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.

title: Information security in project management
description: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

title: Information security in project management integrated
description: Incorporate information security considerations into all stages of project management.

title: Information security in supplier relationships
description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services.

title: Information security management system
description: The organization shall establish, implement, maintain and continually improve an information security management system, including the processes needed and their interactions, in accordance with the requirements of ISO 27001.

title: Information security objective and planning to achieve them
description: The organization shall establish information security objectives at relevant functions and levels. The information security objectives shall: a) be consistent with the information security policy; b) be measurable (if practicable); c) take into account applicable information security requirements, and results from risk assessment and risk treatment; d) be monitored; e) be communicated; f) be updated as appropriate; g) be available as documented information. The organization shall retain documented information on the information security objectives. When planning how to achieve its information security objectives, the organization shall determine: h) what will be done; i) what resources will be required; j) who will be responsible; k) when it will be completed; and l) how the results will be evaluated.

title: Information security policy established
description: Develop and maintain an information security policy that outlines your approach to protecting information assets and systems.

title: Information security program established
description: Establish an information security program for your organization - this security program should include details such as who holds what security positions in the company, what the objectives of the program are, what the SLAs are for your applications, as well as including information about what policies you have in place and how often they are reviewed.

title: Information security risk assessment
description: The organization shall perform information security risk assessments at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed or occur, taking account of the criteria established in 6.1.2 a). The organization shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk assessments.

title: Information security risk treatment
description: The organization shall retain documented information of the results of the information security risk treatment.

title: Information security roles and responsibilities
description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organization needs.

title: Information sharing arrangements implemented
description: Determine the purpose and scope of information sharing, such as exchanging threat intelligence, indicators of compromise, or emerging threat trends. Identify trusted partners, industry groups, or public authorities with whom information can be shared securely.Create formal agreements (e.g., Memorandums of Understanding or Non-Disclosure Agreements) that outline the terms of information sharing, including responsibilities, confidentiality obligations, and usage limitation

title: Infrastructure changes are communicated internally
description: Establish effective communication channels to inform relevant teams and stakeholders about planned infrastructure changes. Internal communication ensures awareness and coordination during change implementation.

title: Intellectual property rights
description: The organization shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.

title: Internal Audit Performed
description: The organization shall conduct internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the information security management system: a) conforms to 1) the organization’s own requirements for its information security management system; 2) the requirements of this document; b) is effectively implemented and maintained.

title: Internal Audit Program
description: The organization shall plan, establish, implement and maintain an audit programme(s), including the frequency, methods, responsibilities, planning requirements and reporting. When establishing the internal audit programme(s), the organization shall consider the importance of the processes concerned and the results of previous audits. The organization shall: a) define the audit criteria and scope for each audit; b) select auditors and conduct audits that ensure objectivity and the impartiality of the audit process; c) ensure that the results of the audits are reported to relevant management; Documented information shall be available as evidence of the implementation of the audit programme(s) and the audit results.

title: Internal privacy policies established
description: Develop an employee privacy policy governing the collection and use of employee data. Update internal privacy notices for employees. Clearly communicate what data is collected, how it's used, and employee rights.Establish a process for regular review and update of these policies.

title: Internal security audit performed
description: An internal security audit is performed annually to evaluate the effectiveness of information security controls, verify compliance with security requirements, and identify areas for improvement.

title: Internal security communication maintained
description: Establish regular communication channels for internal security updates, alerts, and policies to keep employees informed about relevant security practices, potential threats, and compliance requirements.

title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
description: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained.

title: Leadership and commitment
description: Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment with respect to the information security management system by: a) ensuring the information security policy and the information security objectives are established and are compatible with the strategic direction of the organization; b) ensuring the integration of the information security management system requirements into the organization’s processes; c) ensuring that the resources needed for the information security management system are available; d) communicating the importance of effective information security management and of conforming to the information security management system requirements; e) ensuring that the information security management system achieves its intended outcome(s); f) directing and supporting persons to contribute to the effectiveness of the information security management system; g) promoting continual improvement; and h) supporting other relevant management roles to demonstrate their leadership as it applies to their areas of responsibility.

title: Leadership security commitment established
description: Your leadership team, including founders and board members, needs to demonstrate they're serious about security. This means actively overseeing and supporting your company's security efforts, not just delegating it to the tech team.

title: Legal requirements tracked and met
description: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organization's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented and kept up to date.

title: Management responsibilities
description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures of the organization.

title: Management review - General
description: Top management shall review the organization's information security management system at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.

title: Management review implemented
description: Conduct regular management reviews to assess the effectiveness, adequacy, and alignment of the compliance framework with organizational objectives, risks, and regulatory requirements.

title: Management review inputs
description: The management review shall include consideration of: a) the status of actions from previous management reviews; b) changes in external and internal issues that are relevant to the information security management system; c) changes in needs and expectations of interested parties that are relevant to the information security management system; d) feedback on the information security performance, including trends in: 1) nonconformities and corrective actions; 2) monitoring and measurement results; 3) audit results; 4) fulfilment of information security objectives; e) feedback from interested parties; f) results of risk assessment and status of risk treatment plan; g) opportunities for continual improvement.

title: Management review results
description: The results of the management review shall include decisions related to continual improvement opportunities and any needs for changes to the information security management system. Documented information shall be available as evidence of the results of management reviews.

title: Model training data restrictions enforced
description: Implement and maintain technical and procedural controls that prevent customer data from being used in model training without explicit authorization.

title: Network diagrams maintained
description: Maintain an accurate and current visual representation of your organization's network architecture.

title: Non-conformance and corrective action procedures established
description: Systematically identify, document, investigate, and address information security non-conformities through structured corrective actions that prevent recurrence and drive continuous improvement.

title: Nonconformity and corrective action
description: When a Nonconformity occurs, the organization shall: a) React to the nonconformity, and as applicable: 1) take action to control and correct it; 2) deal with the consequences b) evaluate the need for action to eliminate the causes of nonconformity, in order that it does not recur or occur elsewhere, by; 1) reviewing the nonconformity; 2) determining the causes of the nonconformity; and 3) determining if similar nonconformities exist, or could potentially occur c) implement any action needed; d) review the effectiveness of any corrective action taken; and e) make changes to the information security management system, if necessary. Corrective actions shall be appropriate to the effects of the nonconformities encountered. Documented information shall be available as evidence of: f) The nature of the nonconformities and any subsequent actions taken, g) the results of any corrective action.

title: Offboarding process established
description: An offboarding process is established for departing employees to ensure that they are removed from relevant systems and accounts.

title: Onboarding process established
description: An onboarding process is established for new employees to ensure that they are properly trained and equipped to perform their job responsibilities.

title: Operation planning and control
description: The organization shall plan, implement and control the processes needed to meet requirements, and to implement the actions determined in Clause 6, by: — establishing criteria for the processes; — implementing control of the processes in accordance with the criteria. Documented information shall be available to the extent necessary to have confidence that the processes have been carried out as planned. The organization shall control planned changes and review the consequences of unintended changes, taking action to mitigate any adverse effects, as necessary. The organization shall ensure that externally provided processes, products or services that are relevant to the information security management system are controlled.

title: Operational procedures maintained
description: Establish and maintain standardized operating procedures that ensure consistent, secure, and reliable operation of information systems and supporting infrastructure.

title: Organizational roles, responsibilities and authorities
description: Top management shall ensure that the responsibilities and authorities for roles relevant to information security are assigned and communicated within the organization. Top management shall assign the responsibility and authority for: a) ensuring that the information security management system conforms to the requirements of this document; b) reporting on the performance of the information security management system to top management.

title: Outsourced development controls
description: The organization shall direct, monitor and review the activities related to outsourced system development.

title: Password manager used
description: A company-wide password manager is deployed to securely store and share credentials across the organization. All shared accounts and secrets are shared through the password manager using a principle of least privilege.

title: Performance evaluations conducted
description: Conduct regular performance evaluations for employees to assess their job performance, identify areas for improvement, and recognize exceptional contributions. Performance evaluations support talent development and performance management.

title: Physical access restricted
description: Physical access to the organization's facilities, equipment, and systems is restricted to authorized personnel only.

title: Physical entry
description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.

title: Physical security monitoring
description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.

title: Physical security perimeters
description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

title: Physical security policy established
description: Establish a physical security policy that outlines the organization's processes for protecting physical assets and resources.

title: Planning of Changes
description: When the organization determines the need for changes to the information security management system, the changes shall be carried out in a planned manner.

title: Policies Established
description: Top management shall establish an information security policy that: a) is appropriate to the purpose of the organization; b) includes information security objectives (see 6.2) or provides the framework for setting information security objectives; c) includes a commitment to satisfy applicable requirements related to information security; d) includes a commitment to continual improvement of the information security management system. The information security policy shall: e) be available as documented information; f) be communicated within the organization; g) be available to interested parties, as appropriate.

title: Policies for information security
description: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals and if significant changes occur.

title: Policies signed by relevant personnel
description: Security policies are formally acknowledged and signed by all relevant personnel, establishing accountability for security responsibilities.

title: Policy for compelled disclosure from law enforcement established
description: Develop a formal policy outlining procedures for responding to law enforcement requests for data. Establish a process for verifying the authenticity and legality of disclosure requests.

title: Privacy official assigned
description: Designate a privacy official responsible for overseeing the organization's compliance with privacy requirements and managing privacy-related initiatives.

title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
description: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.

title: Protection of records
description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access and unauthorized release.

title: Public facing content review process established
description: Establish a formal process to review publicly accessible systems for Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) and ensure its immediate removal if discovered. This process protects sensitive information from unauthorized disclosure.

title: Public security page available
description: Maintain a public-facing security page that clearly communicates the organization's security posture, practices, and commitments.

title: Recruitment program established
description: Establish a recruitment program designed to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals whose skills and qualifications align with the entity's strategic objectives and operational needs

title: Reference checks performed for employees
description: Reference checks are conducted when hiring new employees to verify their qualifications, experience, and suitability for the role.

title: Regular phishing simulations conducted
description: Conduct regular phishing simulations to assess the effectiveness of your organization in detecting and preventing social engineering attacks. These simulations help evaluate whether existing training and response procedures are functioning as intended, ensuring that employees are prepared to recognize and respond to phishing attempts.

title: Regular self assessments performed
description: Ensure all employees conduct regular self-assessments to review their performance, responsibilities, and effectiveness in achieving organizational objectives.

title: Relevant authorities identified
description: Maintain a clear record of which data protection authorities have jurisdiction over your operations, and establish reliable communication channels with them for compliance reporting and incident response.

title: Remote work policy established
description: Establish a remote work policy that outlines the requirements, guidelines, and best practices for securely working from outside the office. Ensure the policy covers topics such as approved devices, secure network connections, data protection measures, and communication protocols.

title: Remote working controls
description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed or stored outside the organization's premises.

title: Resource Management
description: The organization shall determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance and continual improvement of the information security management system.

title: Resources for ISMS available
description: Demonstrate that sufficient resources are in place to support your ISMS. This can be done by showing external support, internal task tracking, or evidence of security tool usage.

title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.

title: Roles and responsibilities specified
description: Clearly define roles and responsibilities for all employees within the organization. Specifying roles helps establish accountability and ensures that employees understand their duties and expectations.

title: Sanction policy established
description: Establish a sanction policy that outlines the appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the organization's security policies and procedures. Sanctions help enforce accountability and deter employees from engaging in misconduct.

title: Scope for compliance framework established
description: Establish a clearly defined scope for the compliance framework that identifies relevant business processes, IT assets, and organizational boundaries. This scope should align with regulatory, security, and operational requirements, enabling consistent application of compliance controls and efficient management of risk.

title: Secure coding
description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

title: Secure development life cycle
description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system development activities.

title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
description: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented.

title: Security Awareness
description: Persons doing work under the organization’s control shall be aware of: a) the information security policy; b) their contribution to the effectiveness of the information security management system, including the benefits of improved information security performance; and c) the implications of not conforming with the information security management system requirements.

title: Security Violations Managed
description: Establish a security violations management process that outlines the your procedures for detecting, documenting, and addressing security violations by your employees.

title: Security awareness training conducted
description: Security awareness training is conducted annually for all employees, covering cybersecurity threats, social engineering, authentication best practices, and handling of sensitive data.

title: Security official assigned
description: Designate a security official who is responsible for the development, implementation, and oversight of security policies and procedures, as well as management of the incident response process.

title: Security testing in development and acceptance
description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.

title: Segregation of duties
description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.

title: Separation of development
description: Development, testing and production environments shall be separated and secured.

title: Service description communicated
description: Clear and detailed service descriptions are communicated to customers or users, outlining the scope, features, and limitations of the services provided.

title: Software development lifecycle established
description: A well-defined and documented development lifecycle is implemented for software and applications.

title: Statement of applicability completed
description: Complete a formal document identifying relevant controls to your organization based on an ISO guideline.

title: Subscribe to security advisories and alerts
description: Create a system to receive ongoing security alerts, advisories, and directives from trusted sources and share them with your team to address any risks as necessary.

title: Sufficient resources provided
description: Demonstrate that sufficient resources are in place to support your ICT risk management. This can be done by showing external support, internal task tracking, or evidence of security tool usage.

title: Support system available
description: Maintain a support system or helpdesk to assist users with inquiries, technical issues, and incident reporting. A responsive support system enhances user experience and aids in timely issue resolution.

title: System changes externally communicated
description: Communicate relevant system changes, updates, or maintenance activities to external users or customers who may be impacted. External communication helps manage expectations and maintain transparency.

title: System changes internally communicated
description: Ensure that system changes, updates, and maintenance activities are communicated internally to relevant teams and stakeholders. Internal communication helps coordinate efforts and minimize potential disruptions.

title: System documentation controlled and managed
description: Manage system and process documentation under controlled revision and access.

title: Terms and conditions of employment
description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security.

title: Third-party security oversight conducted
description: Third-party security oversight and governance of the organization's security controls is conducted by a qualified provider, ensuring independent verification of control effectiveness and compliance posture.

title: This is a test control
description: **Lorem Ipsum** is simply dummy text of the printing and typesetting industry. Lorem Ipsum has been the industry's standard dummy text ever since the 1500s, when an unknown printer took a galley of type and scrambled it to make a type specimen book.

title: Two‑person control applied to prevent misuse
description: Administer signatures so that any attempted use by someone other than the genuine owner would require collaboration of two or more individuals (e.g., separation of knowledge/possession factors).

title: Understanding the needs of interested parties
description: The organization shall determine: a) interested parties that are relevant to the information security management system; b) the relevant requirements of these interested parties; c) which of these requirements will be addressed through the information security management system.

title: Understanding the organization and its context
description: The organization shall determine external and internal issues that are relevant to its purpose and that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcome(s) of its information security management system.

title: Use of privileged utility programs
description: The use of utility programs that can be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

title: Vendor agreements established
description: Establish clear and well-defined agreements with third-party vendors and partners that outline responsibilities, security expectations, and service-level commitments.

title: Whistleblower policy established
description: Establish a whistleblower policy that allows employees to report any wrongdoing or unethical behavior within the organization anonymously and without fear of retaliation. A whistleblower policy promotes transparency and accountability.

title: Working in secure areas
description: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.

title: Workstation use and security policy established
description: Establish a workstation use and security policy that outlines the appropriate use of workstations and security measures to protect them from unauthorized access. The policy helps safeguard the organization's assets and data.

title: Zero data retention agreements established
description: Establish and maintain formal agreements with all downstream model providers and data processors that mandate zero data retention after processing.

title: AI impact assessment performed
description: Assess the potential consequences for individuals, groups of individuals, and societies that can result from the development, deployment, and use of AI systems.

title: AI risk assessment performed
description: Define and apply an AI risk assessment process to identify, analyze, and evaluate risks associated with AI systems throughout their lifecycle.

title: AI supply chain managed
description: Establish processes to ensure that AI-related services, products, and materials from suppliers align with the organization's responsible AI principles and requirements.

title: Compliance policy established
description: Establish and maintain a comprehensive compliance policy that defines how the organization identifies, monitors, and fulfills its regulatory, legal, and contractual obligations.

title: Cybersecurity insurance maintained
description: Maintain cybersecurity insurance coverage to mitigate financial risks associated with cyber incidents, data breaches, and other security-related events. Cybersecurity insurance can provide financial protection and support recovery efforts.

title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) completed
description: Identify processing activities involving personal data that may pose high risks to individuals' privacy rights.To determine whether you need to complete a full DPIA, complete the below questionnaire in the 'recommended templates' section. If you answer "Yes" to any of the questions, you need to fill out a DPIA.You can find a template for the DPIA here: https://gdpr.eu/data-protection-impact-assessment-template/

title: Exit strategies for critical vendors established
description: Establish formal exit strategies for critical third-party ICT Vendors to ensure continuity of operations in the event of provider termination or disruption.

title: FDA non‑repudiation certification maintained
description: Certify to FDA that electronic signatures used are the legally binding equivalent of handwritten signatures, and provide additional certification/testimony upon request.

title: Gap asessments performed
description: Perform regular assessments to identify and document gaps in your compliance program. These assessments help ensure your security controls remain effective and highlight areas that need improvement or updating.

title: General actions to address risks and opportunities
description: When planning for the information security management system, the organization shall consider the issues referred to in 4.1 and the requirements referred to in 4.2 and determine the risks and opportunities that need to be addressed to: a) ensure the information security management system can achieve its intended outcome(s); b) prevent, or reduce, undesired effects; c) achieve continual improvement. The organization shall plan: d) actions to address these risks and opportunities; and e) how to 1) integrate and implement the actions into its information security management system processes; and 2) evaluate the effectiveness of these actions

title: Internal audit program defined
description: Establish an internal audit program to evaluate the effectiveness and compliance of the organization's Information Security Management System (ISMS).

title: Outsourced development secured
description: Implement measures to ensure the security of software development activities outsourced to third-party vendors.

title: Risk assessments performed
description: Risk assessments are conducted at least annually to identify and evaluate potential threats and vulnerabilities that could impact the organization's assets.

title: Risk management policy established
description: A risk management policy is established that outlines the organization's approach to identifying, assessing, and mitigating information security risks.

title: Risk owners identified
description: Assign ownership of identified risks to specific individuals within the organization to ensure accountability and effective risk management. Risk owners are responsible for monitoring, mitigating, and reporting on the risks assigned to them, enabling proactive management and alignment with organizational objectives.

title: Risk treatment plans established
description: Establish and maintain risk treatment plans to systematically address identified security risks through a documented approach of mitigation strategies, ensuring proper oversight and continuous monitoring of risk reduction efforts.

title: Software supply chain risks monitored
description: Software supply chain risks are monitored by assessing the security of third-party libraries and open-source components used in the organization's software development lifecycle.

title: Targeted risk analysis conducted
description: Based on your risk assessment methodology developed in your ‘Risk Management Policy’, implement a more targeted risk analyses specifically considering the following points:- Evaluate which system components are not at risk for malware- Frequency of periodic malware scans- Review application and system account access privileges- Frequency of password/passphrase changes for application and system accounts- Frequency of periodic log reviews for certain system components- Frequency of installing security patches/updates- Frequency of change- and tamper-detection mechanism evaluations for payment pages- Frequency of periodic training for incident response personnelDocument the results of each targeted risk analysis, including the rationale for the determined frequencies or methodologies. Review and update your targeted risk analyses at least once every 12 months and upon significant changes to the environment, with approval by senior management for the targeted risk analyses and resulting decisions.

title: Vendor inventory maintained
description: An accurate and up-to-date inventory of all vendors is maintained, including details such as the services provided, contract terms, and the scope of access they have.

title: Vendor management policy established
description: Develop and maintain a third-party management policy that defines the your approach to managing information security risks associated with external parties.

title: Vendor management policy established
description: A vendor management policy is established to assess, monitor, and manage the risks associated with third-party vendors, ensuring that external partners meet security and compliance standards.

title: Vendor risk manager appointed
description: Appoint a dedicated Third-Party Risk Manager to oversee the evaluation, monitoring, and management of risks associated with critical third-party ICT Vendors. This role ensures that third-party relationships align with regulatory requirements and organizational resilience objectives.

title: Vendors classified by data sensitivity
description: Classify Vendors based on their risk level to the organization's data and operations. This classification helps prioritize risk assessments, security reviews, and appropriate security controls for each Vendor.

title: Vendors classified by risk level
description: Classify Vendors based on the sensitivity of the data they handle or process on behalf of the organization. This classification enables proper data protection measures and compliance requirements for different types of data shared with Vendors.

title: Automated software patch management performed
description: Automate the process of deploying software patches and updates to systems and applications. Automated patch management helps ensure that critical security patches are applied promptly to address known vulnerabilities.

title: Bug bounty program used
description: Implement a bug bounty program to incentivize security researchers and ethical hackers to responsibly report security vulnerabilities. This program can help identify and address security weaknesses before they are exploited maliciously.

title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained, the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities should be evaluated and appropriate measures should be taken.

title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.

title: Penetration testing findings remediated
description: Vulnerabilities identified during penetration testing are promptly remediated.

title: Penetration testing performed within the last 12 months
description: Penetration testing is conducted at least every 12 months to identify potential vulnerabilities in the organization's systems, applications, and infrastructure.

title: Protection against malware
description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.

title: Removable electronic media scanned for malware
description: Assess your current anti-malware solution and check if it supports automatic scanning or continuous behavioral analysis of removable media. If not, consider upgrading or adding a solution that does.Configure and test your anti-malware solution to do at least ONE of the following:- Perform automatic scans when removable media is inserted, connected, or mounted.- Conduct continuous behavioral analysis of systems or processes when removable media is connected.Set up your anti-malware solution to log all scan activities and results. Schedule periodic reviews of your anti-malware configurations.

title: Reporting channel for open-source vulnerabilities
description: Establish a dedicated reporting channel for open-source software vulnerabilities. This allows external researchers or users to responsibly report security issues related to open-source components used in the organization's applications and infrastructure.

title: Threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) completed
description: Conduct advanced threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) on critical ICT systems to simulate real-world cyberattacks.

title: Vulnerabilities remediated
description: Detected vulnerabilities are promptly remediated to minimize the risk of exploitation. This includes establishing clear protocols for prioritizing vulnerabilities based on severity and ensuring timely resolution of critical security issues.

title: Vulnerabilities scanned
description: Regular vulnerability scans are conducted on systems and applications to identify potential security flaws. This includes automated scanning tools that systematically examine infrastructure, applications, and code repositories for known vulnerabilities.

title: Vulnerability management policy acknowledged by employees
description: All employees have acknowledged and agreed to the vulnerability management policy. This policy outlines the procedures for identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities in the organization's systems and applications.

title: Vulnerability management policy established
description: A vulnerability management policy is established that outlines the procedures for identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities in the organization's systems and applications.
